name: Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena
name_original: Laguna de Sariñena — circular desde Sariñena
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Sariñena traverse the unique Monegros landscape, characterized by arid terrain and ochre colors. This region features distinctive natural formations such as tozales, which are isolated hills sculpted by erosion. The area also includes the Laguna de Sariñena, offering a different natural attraction within the broader arid environment. These features provide a varied backdrop for cycling adventures. Best touring cycling routes around Sariñena The most popular touring cycling route is Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena, a 51.8 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 7 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the expansive Monegros landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena, an easy 15.8 miles (25.4 km) path. This route offers a gentler ride around the natural lagoon. Local touring cyclists also love the From Castejón de Monegros to Miramón — circular route, a 12.9 miles (20.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Sariñena is defined by the arid Los Monegros landscape, unique tozales, and the Laguna de Sariñena. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging routes. The routes in Sariñena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from more than 6 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sariñena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sariñena?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes available around Sariñena, offering a diverse range of experiences through the unique Monegros landscape. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging expeditions.

Q: What makes the landscape around Sariñena unique for touring cyclists?
A: The region around Sariñena is characterized by the unique Monegros landscape, featuring arid terrain and striking ochre colors. Cyclists will encounter distinctive natural formations such as &lt;strong&gt;tozales&lt;/strong&gt; (isolated hills sculpted by erosion) and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980280092/laguna-de-sarinena-circular-from-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;, providing an almost otherworldly backdrop for your rides.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for touring cycling routes in Sariñena?
A: Sariñena offers a range of touring cycling routes to suit different abilities. You'll find 2 easy routes, 13 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both casual riders and experienced cyclists can find a suitable challenge.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980280092/laguna-de-sarinena-circular-from-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Sariñena — circular from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 25.4 km path that offers a gentler ride around the natural lagoon, mostly on paved surfaces.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes in Sariñena?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379789762/vuelta-al-monegros-circular-desde-sarinena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Monegros — circular desde Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 83.3 km journey with significant elevation gain, exploring the expansive and rugged Monegros landscape.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes around Sariñena are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379793520/from-castejon-de-monegros-to-miramon-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Castejón de Monegros to Miramón — circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379795104/cartuja-de-monegros-and-sarinena-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartuja de Monegros and Sariñena Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling around Sariñena?
A: The routes often pass by impressive natural landmarks. You can encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; which offers 360-degree views, and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit)&lt;/a&gt;, a small stone monolith marking a high point on some routes.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, some routes may lead you near historical sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379795104/cartuja-de-monegros-and-sarinena-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartuja de Monegros and Sariñena Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historic Cartuja de Monegros. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5184605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sijena Monastery&lt;/a&gt; is another notable religious building in the wider area that can be explored.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Sariñena?
A: The touring cycling routes in Sariñena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from more than 6 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique arid landscapes, the solitary atmosphere, and the distinctive geological formations like the tozales that make for a memorable riding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sariñena?
A: Given the arid nature of the Monegros landscape, the best times for touring cycling in Sariñena are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, temperatures are more moderate, making for a more comfortable ride compared to the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Sariñena?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints that provide stunning panoramas of the Monegros landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; is known for its spectacular 360-degree views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bujal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers excellent vistas of the unique terrain.
tips: The capital of the Monegros, the name of Sariñena means "the one with the most amount of panizo (millet) farmland".
About 70 kilometers from Zaragoza and within the arid landscape of Los Monegros, is one of the largest lagoons in Spain and one of the best for observing water birds in Aragon.
